lever: de-delegate by porting networkx's SPFA Bellman-Ford (queue that re-relaxes only improved nodes, skips a node while any predecessor still queued; pred is a dict of predecessor LISTS, ties append) in-process on a one-time native to_dict_of_dicts snapshot — skips the full fnx->nx conversion that made fnx 6.85x slower. nx's own naive loop was 128x slower; the SPFA variant is what makes nx fast. heuristic=False (public default) so the heuristic recent_update block is skipped.

parity.coverage: directed+undirected, negative weights, tie-heavy, unweighted; pred dict-of-lists + dist byte-exact to nx

headline: bellman_ford_predecessor_and_distance: 6.85x slower than nx -> ~1.5x near-parity, ~4x self-speedup, byte-exact (SPFA port on native snapshot). Pure Python, no rebuild. MultiGraph/callable-weight keep nx delegation.
followup: negative_edge_cycle (6.16x, uses bellman-ford internally) — assess next
